name: Video element auditory content has captions
description: This rule checks that captions are available for audio information in non-streaming `video` elements.
applicability_text: This rule applies to every [non-streaming](#non-streaming-media-element) `video` element that is [visible][] where the video contains [audio](#audio-output) that is not only silence.
expectations:
For each test target, audio information that is not conveyed visually in the video, is available through [captions](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#dfn-captions).

**Note:** Captions can be either embedded in the video file itself or can be made available trough a separate track.

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: A video element that has open captions for all of the audio content.

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: A video element with an associated track element that contains captions for all of the audio content.

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: A video element without any form of captions.

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: A video element with an associated track element that contains incorrect captions. Specifically, the captions flip the role of the mouse and computer by saying, "Not being able to use your mouse because your computer doesn't work is frustrating."

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: A video element with text on the same page that describes the audio in the video.
examples.code: The video below shows a man working on a keyboard. A voiceover is heard saying the following text: Web accessibility perspectives. Keyboard compatibility. Not being able to use your computer because your mouse doesn't work, is frustrating. Many people use only the keyboard to navigate websites. Either through preference or circumstance.

examples.name: Failed Example 4
examples.description: A video element with an explicitly associated text on the same page that describes the audio in the video.
examples.code: The video below shows a man working on a keyboard. A voiceover is heard saying the following text: Web accessibility perspectives. Keyboard compatibility. Not being able to use your computer because your mouse doesn't work, is frustrating. Many people use only the keyboard to navigate websites. Either through preference or circumstance.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: A video element that is not [visible][].

assumptions: This rule assumes that the video element is used to play a video (for example, not only used to display an image), and that there is a mechanism to start the video.
accessibility_support: There are no accessibility support issues known.
